definition: Root ODPR workflow document that declares metadata, recipe version, type, execution policy, context policy, inputs, outputs, steps, gates, review expectations, and lightweight run policy.
doUseFor: Declaring repeatable data product workflows
Making SDK automation inspectable
Describing CI, release, localization, hybrid, or agent-safe workflows
Using one portable document for validation, dry-run, execution, or resume
doNotUseFor: Storing SDK invocation mode, dry-run responses, run manifests, logs, readiness checks, or write-scope results

definition: Root ODPR provider profile document that gives a stable providerRef target a provider family, model, provider class, endpoint reference, credentials reference, and safe runtime defaults.
doUseFor: Standardizing named provider profiles used by recipes
Declaring model and provider family defaults
Keeping provider configuration portable across SDKs, CI/CD systems, MCP servers, and platforms
doNotUseFor: Embedding raw API keys or secrets

definition: Root ODPR metadata-only discovery document that lists available recipes, optionally organizes entries into catalog groups, and points to complete Recipe files.
doUseFor: Discovering available recipes
Organizing recipe entries with optional recipeCatalog.groups and groupRef
Indexing recipe metadata for agents and SDKs
Pointing to complete Recipe files before loading and validating them
doNotUseFor: Storing runtime status, planned writes, run ids, logs, provider readiness, or write-scope checks

definition: One declared operation in an ODPR recipe, such as generate, validate, odpc.build, odpg.build, odpg.render, portfolio.build, portfolio.refresh, portfolio.sync, portfolio.localize, portfolio.render, portfolio.explain, or explain.
doUseFor: Ordering SDK or platform operations
Declaring command-specific with parameters
Overriding provider references for one LLM-backed operation
doNotUseFor: Reusable step fragments through uses/template/from/when/foreach/matrix in v1

definition: Workflow intent that declares whether a recipe expects local, hosted, hybrid, or model-free runtime/provider class.
doUseFor: Separating development from production execution
Declaring local, hosted, hybrid, or none runtime/provider class
Referencing a default provider profile for LLM-backed steps
doNotUseFor: Selecting SDK invocation mode such as validate, dry-run, execute, or resume

definition: Policy for preferred workflow context format such as YAML, TOON, GCF, or automatic fallback.
doUseFor: Declaring compact context preferences
Preferring GCF or TOON sidecars when available
Making prompt and review context explicit
doNotUseFor: Embedding full document bodies in recipes

definition: Validation, quality, review, or publication condition that must be visible to recipe runners and agents.
doUseFor: Declaring validation requirements
Making human review explicit
Representing CI or release checks
doNotUseFor: Storing approval records, signatures, workflow pauses, or gate runtime status
